resources.string: Add at least 2 entries to see your chart
Stand tall. Slowly roll your head in a full circle — forward, side, back, side. Switch direction halfway through.
Extend your arms out to the sides. Make small circles, gradually increasing to large circles. Reverse halfway.
Start feet together, arms at sides. Jump feet apart while raising arms overhead. Jump back to start. Repeat quickly.
Run in place, driving your knees up to hip height. Pump your arms. Keep your core tight and stay on the balls of your feet.
Stand shoulder-width apart. Push hips back and bend knees as if sitting. Lower until thighs are parallel, then stand back up.
Step forward with one leg and lower your hips until both knees are at 90°. Push back to start. Alternate legs.
On all fours, arch your back up (cat), then drop your belly and lift your chest (cow). Move slowly with your breath.
Hold a push-up position on your forearms. Keep your body in a straight line from head to heels. Engage your core throughout.
Start with a light, easy-paced jog. Focus on loosening up your body and finding a comfortable breathing rhythm.
Jog in place while kicking your heels up to touch your glutes. Keep a quick pace and stay light on your feet.
Sprint at maximum effort for a short burst, then jog or walk to recover. Repeat. Push hard during each sprint.
Get into a low athletic stance. Shuffle sideways quickly, staying low. Switch direction at the midpoint.
Walk at an easy pace. Let your heart rate come down gradually. Take deep breaths and relax your muscles.
Stand tall with feet together, arms at sides. Spread your toes, engage your legs, lengthen your spine. Breathe deeply.
From all fours, lift hips up and back to form an inverted V. Press your heels toward the floor. Relax your head.
Step one foot back. Bend front knee to 90°. Raise arms overhead, palms facing. Square your hips forward.
From warrior I, open hips and arms to the side. Gaze over your front hand. Keep front knee bent at 90°.
Stand on one leg. Place the other foot on your inner thigh or calf (not knee). Hands at heart center or overhead.
Lie face-down. Place hands under shoulders and press up, lifting your chest. Keep hips on the floor. Look slightly upward.
Kneel and sit back on your heels. Fold forward, extending your arms ahead on the floor. Rest your forehead down. Breathe deeply.
Lie flat on your back, arms at sides with palms up. Close your eyes. Relax every muscle and focus on your breathing.
Stand, drop into a squat, kick feet back to plank, do a push-up, jump feet forward, then jump up with arms overhead.
In plank position, drive your knees alternately toward your chest as fast as you can. Keep your core tight.
Perform a regular squat, then explode up into a jump. Land softly and immediately lower into the next squat.
Start in plank with arms straight. Lower your body until your chest nearly touches the floor. Push back up. Keep body straight.
Start in plank position. Jump your feet apart and then back together, like a horizontal jumping jack. Keep core stable.
Stand and jump as high as you can, pulling your knees up to your chest at the top. Land softly and repeat immediately.
Lie on your back. Lift shoulders off the floor. Alternate bringing each elbow to the opposite knee in a pedaling motion.
Sit on edge of a chair. Place hands beside your hips. Slide off and lower your body by bending elbows to 90°. Push back up.
Lie on your back, knees bent, feet flat. Lift your hips toward the ceiling, squeezing your glutes. Lower slowly.
Lie face-down with arms extended forward. Simultaneously lift your arms, chest, and legs off the floor. Hold, then lower.
Stand with your back flat against a wall. Slide down until your knees are at 90°. Hold this position. Keep your back pressed to the wall.
Jump laterally from one foot to the other, swinging your arms. Land softly on each foot. Stay low and keep moving.
Jump up explosively, spreading arms and legs into a star shape at the top. Land with feet together and repeat.
Step one foot onto a raised surface, then the other. Step back down. Alternate the leading foot each time.
Skip rope at a quick pace (or simulate without a rope). Stay on the balls of your feet, wrists turning the rope.
From boxing stance: throw a quick jab with your lead hand, followed by a powerful cross with your rear hand. Rotate hips.
Throw a hook by swinging your arm in a curve at head height, then follow with an uppercut driving your fist upward from below.
Bend your knees to duck low, then shift your weight side to side, rising back up. Imagine dodging punches.
Throw combinations of punches at an imaginary opponent. Move around, stay light on your feet. Mix jabs, crosses, hooks.
Practice moving forward, backward, and side to side in boxing stance. Stay balanced, keep guard up, and move on the balls of your feet.
Throw rapid straight punches alternating hands as fast as you can. Keep your guard up and stay balanced.
Practice blocking, parrying, and slipping punches. Keep hands up to protect your face. React to imaginary attacks.
Sit with legs extended. Reach forward toward your toes, keeping your back as straight as possible. Hold the stretch.
Stand on one leg. Grab your other foot behind you and pull it toward your glutes. Keep knees together. Hold and switch.
Kneel in a lunge position. Push your hips forward until you feel a stretch in the front of your back hip. Hold and switch.
Bring one arm across your chest. Use the other hand to gently press it closer. Feel the stretch in your shoulder. Switch sides.
Sit with legs extended. Exhale and fold forward from the hips, reaching for your feet. Relax your head and hold.
From all fours, bring one knee forward and lay that shin on the floor. Extend the other leg back. Fold forward over the front leg.
Sit with knees bent, lean back slightly. Twist your torso from side to side, touching the floor beside your hip each time.
Lie on your back, legs straight. Keeping legs together, lift them up to 90° then lower slowly without touching the floor.
Lie on your back, arms up, knees bent at 90°. Extend opposite arm and leg outward simultaneously. Return and switch sides.
Lie on your side. Prop yourself up on your forearm. Lift your hips into a straight line. Hold. Keep hips from sagging.
Lie on your back, hands under your hips. Lift legs slightly and alternate kicking up and down in small, rapid movements.
Start with an easy, low-resistance pedaling pace. Get your legs warmed up and find a comfortable cadence.
Increase resistance and lower your cadence. Stand in the pedals if needed. Simulate climbing a steep hill.
Maintain a steady, moderate pace. Focus on consistent effort and breathing. Build cardiovascular endurance.
Reduce resistance to minimum. Pedal slowly and let your heart rate come down. Gentle spin to recover.
Practice freestyle stroke technique. Focus on arm pull, body rotation, and rhythmic breathing to each side.
Practice backstroke technique. Lie on your back, alternate arm pulls overhead. Keep hips high and kick steadily.
Stay vertical in the water. Use eggbeater kicks or scissors kicks with sculling arms to keep your head above water.
Swim at a relaxed, easy pace. Focus on smooth strokes and deep breathing. Let your muscles recover gradually.
